content.claim: Free school choice in Hungary (instituted to allow students to enroll outside residential districts subject to capacity limits) is an independently documented driver of compositional school sorting. Kertesi and Kézdi research found that school segregation is most strongly predicted by the proportion of Roma students in a town, and that school-switching behavior significantly alters school ethnic composition, diminishing the role of residential distribution.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: These findings document school-choice-driven sorting independent of SEN classification mechanisms
the interaction between school-choice sorting and SEN-classification sorting (whether SEN serves as a complementary or substitutive segregation channel) is not modeled in cited sources
parental perception and prejudice against Roma pupils are cited as mechanisms but not independently quantified
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
